# Book Interventions

This project area asks whether books can function as psychological interventions. Popular nonfiction and psychology books often promise insight, motivation, or transformation, but those effects are rarely tested with the same care as laboratory or clinical interventions.

## Core question

Can reading a book produce measurable changes in prosociality, empathy, compassion, polarization, or motivation to help others?

## Possible designs

- Randomized reading interventions
- Brief excerpt studies
- Longitudinal follow-ups
- Mechanism tests comparing narrative transportation, reflection, and identity change

## Next actions

- Identify candidate books and excerpts.
- Map likely mechanisms.
- Select outcome measures.
- Develop feasible student project versions.
